title: MSA with weak IP indemnity and overbroad liability cap

input.contract_text: Vendor's total liability arising out of or related to the Agreement will not exceed the fees paid by Customer in the three (3) months before the claim. This cap applies to all claims, including confidentiality breaches, data security incidents, payment obligations, and indemnification claims. Vendor disclaims any obligation to defend Customer against third-party claims alleging that the Services infringe intellectual property rights. Customer will indemnify Vendor for all claims arising from Customer's use of the Services.

expected_catches.clause: fees paid ... in the three (3) months before the claim
expected_catches.why: A three-month fees cap is too low for a SaaS vendor handling meaningful customer risk.

expected_catches.clause: cap applies to ... confidentiality breaches, data security incidents
expected_catches.why: Confidentiality and security breaches should be uncapped or subject to a meaningful super-cap.

expected_catches.clause: disclaims any obligation to defend ... intellectual property rights
expected_catches.why: Vendor should provide IP infringement defense and indemnity for its services.

expected_catches.clause: Customer will indemnify Vendor for all claims
expected_catches.why: Customer indemnity is broad and one-sided without reciprocal vendor obligations.

negative_checks.description: The eval should not demand unlimited liability for all ordinary contract claims.
